input: We build an AI system that performs social scoring of citizens across multiple unrelated life domains and feeds a trustworthiness score into housing and benefit allocation decisions.

expected.notes: Article 5(1)(c) — evaluating or classifying natural persons based on their social behaviour or known/predicted personal characteristics leading to detrimental or unfavourable treatment in social contexts unrelated to the original collection context, or unjustified/disproportionate to the gravity of the conduct. Lexicon hits: 'social scoring', 'trustworthiness score'. The 'benefit eligibility' phrasing also lexically matches Annex III.5 essential services (which would otherwise be high-risk), but Article 5 prohibition supersedes the high-risk classification — the domain hit is retained for transparency, high_risk computed false. Cite: EUR-Lex Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, Article 5(1)(c).
